description:
<description>
        You are the release manager of a small SaaS company.  
        Three INI-formatted service configuration files have already been placed in  
        /home/user/release/configs/:

        1. /home/user/release/configs/frontend.ini  
        2. /home/user/release/configs/backend.ini  
        3. /home/user/release/configs/worker.ini  

        Your goal is to aggregate the information from every section of every *.ini* file
        and produce a single, human-readable deployment summary file.

        Detailed requirements
        ---------------------

        1. Create the directory /home/user/release/reports/ if it does not yet exist.  
           (The path must be exactly as written.)

        2. Inside that directory create the file  
           /home/user/release/reports/deployment_summary.log

        3. The file *must* contain exactly six lines, one line for every   
           “service/environment” combination found in the existing INI files  
           (production + staging for each of the three services).

        4. Fixed output line format (this will be validated byte-for-byte):

             service: <SERVICE-NAME>; env: <ENV>; version: <VERSION>; port: <PORT>

           • There is a single leading literal string “service: ” (note the space after the colon).  
           • All field-labels (“service”, “env”, “version”, “port”) must appear in lowercase.  
           • A semicolon followed by exactly one space separates each field.  
           • Lines are terminated with a single Unix newline (LF, \n). NO trailing spaces.

        5. Sort order *must* be:
           • First: env=production lines (alphabetically by service name).  
           • Second: env=staging   lines (alphabetically by service name).

           The automated tests rely on this order, so do not deviate.

        6. Aside from creating the directory and the summary file, do not modify
           any of the existing files. Do not create any extra files.

        When you are done, you can print the path of the summary file to the console
        so a human observer can quickly cat it, but the grading script will only
        check the existence and exact content of /home/user/release/reports/deployment_summary.log.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
        Initial file system state provided to the agent
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/release/configs/frontend.ini"><![CDATA[
[production]
version = 5.4.2
port = 8080

[staging]
version = 5.4.2-rc1
port = 8081
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/release/configs/backend.ini"><![CDATA[
[production]
version = 2.18.0
port = 9090

[staging]
version = 2.18.0-rc1
port = 9091
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/release/configs/worker.ini"><![CDATA[
[production]
version = 1.3.7
port = 7070

[staging]
version = 1.3.7-rc2
port = 7071
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        Expected end state for automated validation
    -->
    <expected_end_state>
        <directory path="/home/user/release/reports" exists="true" />

        <file path="/home/user/release/reports/deployment_summary.log"><![CDATA[
service: backend; env: production; version: 2.18.0; port: 9090
service: frontend; env: production; version: 5.4.2; port: 8080
service: worker; env: production; version: 1.3.7; port: 7070
service: backend; env: staging; version: 2.18.0-rc1; port: 9091
service: frontend; env: staging; version: 5.4.2-rc1; port: 8081
service: worker; env: staging; version: 1.3.7-rc2; port: 7071
]]></file>
    </expected_end_state>
name: task_000000_641e376a
